summary: Decent soundboard recording (a bit thin on bass) captures a scorching July 4 1990 show highlighted by a blistering Scarlet Begonias > Fire on the Mountain stretch and Brent’s soaring Just A Little Light.
review: The performance is remembered as one of the best summer‑’90 shows, with hot‑weather energy fueling standout moments like the extended Scarlet Begonias/Fire on the Mountain sequence, a tight Victim → Foolish Heart → Just A Little Light trio, and an unforgettable rendition of The Wheel.  The Charlie‑remastered soundboard tape is clear overall but can feel thin on modest systems and has occasional vocal drop‑outs; nevertheless it remains listenable and well‑balanced for most listeners.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers praise the show’s setlist, energy, and Brent’s keyboard work; recording issues are noted but are minor, leading to an overall strong rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Remastered by Charlie – clear overall but low on small systems, thin bass, occasional spotty vocals and minor flaws; still a solid tape for the era.

standout_songs: Scarlet Begonias > Fire on the Mountain
show_quality.setlist_flow: Hot July 4th atmosphere fuels a strong first set and an especially powerful third quarter of the second set (Victim → Foolish Heart → Just A Little Light → Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain).

band_member_comments.Jerry: Wore shorts, delivered energetic guitar work; fans repeatedly express love for his playing.
band_member_comments.Phil: Also in shorts; bass solid and pre‑drums segment praised as excellent.
band_member_comments.Bob: His song Victim (or Crime) highlighted as a key moment early in the second set.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent receives high praise – called a beast, delivers a standout Just A Little Light and drives Fire on the Mountain; many consider this his final great performance.
band_member_comments.Drums: No specific critique beyond noting the drum segment was standard but well‑played.
